denial_letter_text:
Dear Ms. Member,

Cigna has completed a medical necessity review of the request for temporomandibular joint total joint replacement. After review, the request is denied.

The clinical criteria for surgical management of the temporomandibular joint are not met. Under the applicable MCG criteria, surgical treatment requires documentation that the request satisfies the threshold requirements of the referenced criteria set. Conservative and non-surgical measures are expected to be addressed before surgical treatment is considered under these criteria, and based on our review the threshold requirements are not satisfied.

In addition, treatment of the temporomandibular joint is subject to a plan exclusion under the terms of this plan. Services that fall under a plan exclusion are not eligible for coverage, and an exclusion applies regardless of the clinical circumstances of an individual request.

If you disagree with this determination, you may file an internal appeal by contacting Member Services at the number on your identification card within the timeframe stated in your plan documents. You may submit additional clinical information with your appeal. A clinician who was not involved in this determination will review your appeal, and you may include any additional records you believe are relevant to your request.

This determination addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
Cigna Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 49-year-old woman with end-stage degenerative temporomandibular joint disease, with bone-on-bone changes and condylar resorption on CT, a maximal interincisal opening of 18 mm, and chronic disabling jaw pain with difficulty eating. She completed more than a year of documented conservative care, including a stabilization splint, physical therapy, anti-inflammatory medication, and two arthrocentesis procedures, all submitted with the request, without lasting relief; her oral and maxillofacial surgeon requested alloplastic total joint replacement. The denial cites MCG criteria for surgical management without naming the specific edition or module, so the conservative-care threshold it references cannot be tested even though that care was already completed. It separately invokes a plan exclusion for temporomandibular joint treatment. The member's plan is fully insured in West Virginia, a state that mandates coverage for the diagnosis and surgical treatment of temporomandibular joint disorders; because the plan is fully insured rather than ERISA self-funded, the state mandate controls over the conflicting plan exclusion.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field; end-stage TMJ DJD is msk; alloplastic TMJ total joint replacement realistic; guideline_mis_cite (vague MCG) is the rationale; 49F in 41-55.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Cigna denying TMJ total joint replacement on vague MCG criteria plus a plan exclusion is realistic; the WV fully-insured state-mandate preemption and completed conservative care are genuine exploitable gaps.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Authentic Cigna tells: cites MCG and a conservative-treatment-first expectation; correct branding; no wrong-insurer fingerprints.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy guideline_mis_cite: MCG cited without naming module, circular threshold assertion, vague conservative-measures language plus a second exclusion ground; exploitable.

clinical_realism.reasoning: 49F end-stage TMJ DJD with condylar resorption, 18 mm opening, year-plus staged conservative care; alloplastic TJR is the correct surgical indication; no biology violations.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Alloplastic TMJ total joint replacement is FDA-cleared standard care for end-stage TMJ degenerative joint disease; exact match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: End-stage TMJ DJD requiring total joint replacement is distinct from the TMJ-arthroscopy/internal-derangement neighbour in stage, ICD subcode, procedure invasiveness, and flaw type.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.098 (nearest=case_151_aetna_priorauth), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Routine TMJ surgery dispute; no banned topics; adult 49F; Cigna fully-insured commercial with no Medicare/Medicaid framing.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender, diagnosis, treatment, funding stable across documents; unmet-threshold/exclusion vs completed care and controlling mandate is appealability not contradiction.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal insurer boilerplate and dense clinical recitation; no GPT vocabulary, listicles, or meta-commentary.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Cigna register with bureaucratic vagueness; clinical context plain factual, no pleading.

financial_auditor.reasoning: No cost/liability or dollar figures; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: Internal appeal present; omitted external-review/ERISA language and buried window are realistic attackable imperfections; no hallucinated statutes; no Medicare framing.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 49F with end-stage degenerative TMJ disease is epidemiologically textbook (female predominance, middle age); consistent female pronouns/'Ms.'; no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna in-scope, fully-insured commercial, adult 49, member-facing internal appeal only, no live-filing language.

date_sanity.reasoning: No contradictions; relative timing (more than a year of conservative care) consistent but no absolute calendar anchor.
date_sanity.improvement: Add absolute 2026 dates and a numeric appeal window.

citation_traceability.reasoning: MCG correctly attributed as vendor but cited generically with no module/edition; plan exclusion described generically; no hallucinated alphanumeric code.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Two independently rebuttable pillars: untestable vague-MCG criterion already satisfied by completed conservative care, and a plan exclusion preempted by WV's TMJ mandate for a fully-insured plan; easy multi-vector appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: MCG criteria cited without naming the specific edition or module, so the conservative-care threshold cannot be tested even though that care was completed
Plan exclusion overridden by West Virginia's state mandate for TMJ surgical coverage because the plan is fully insured, not ERISA self-funded
Documented standard of care (year-plus conservative care, end-stage degenerative disease, 18 mm opening) effectively ignored
strong_defenses: The letter does offer the required appeal pathway (independent non-involved clinician review, ability to submit additional records)
